reason: Blade Runner is gritty, dystopian sci-fi with philosophical depth, no fantasy elements.
Aliens blends intense space horror and survival in a hard sci-fi setting.
The Terminator melds time-travel tech with relentless realism, a hallmark of hard sci-fi.
Escape from New York offers a bleak, near-future vision without any fantasy trappings.
The Abyss features grounded underwater sci-fi and cutting-edge effects.
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan focuses on cloning technology and moral sci-fi themes.
Brazil presents a dark bureaucratic dystopia, firmly in the realm of hard science fiction.
The Right Stuff chronicles the realistic early space program, no fantasy elements.
Back to the Future offers time-travel grounded in pseudo-science, sans fantasy.
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock explores mind-transfer tech in a serious sci-fi context.
